post.description: Creates a new agent with staging version. The agent will be created with an initial staging version that can later be promoted to production.

get.description: Lists all active agents for the specified location. locationId is required parameter to ensure optimal performance. Supports pagination using limit and offset. Optionally filter by isPublished=true to return only agents with a published production version.

parameters.name: isPublished
parameters.description: Optional filter to return only agents with a published production version

patch.description: Updates a specific agent version by versionId. Supports updating nodes, edges, variables, and configuration.
Updates agent metadata such as name, description, and status.

delete.description: Deletes an agent and all its versions.

get.description: Gets a specific agent by its ID for the specified location with all its versions. Returns complete agent metadata and all non-deleted versions (draft, staging, production). locationId is required parameter. The agent must have active status.

404.description: Agent not found or not available

post.description: Promotes a draft version to production.

400.description: Bad Request - Only draft versions can be promoted

post.description:
Executes the specified agent and returns a non-streaming JSON response with the complete agent output. The agent must be in active status and belong to the specified location. locationId is required in the request body. 

**Session Management:**
- For the first message in a new session, do not include the `executionId` in the request payload.
- The API will return an `executionId` along with the agent response, which uniquely identifies this conversation session.
- To continue the conversation within the same session, include the `executionId` from the previous response in subsequent requests. This allows the agent to maintain conversation context and history across multiple interactions.

400.description: Agent is not active or invalid request - locationId is required

403.description: User does not have required scopes to execute this agent

get.description:
**Deprecated endpoint - use GET /agent instead.**

Lists all active agents that have a published production version for the specified location. locationId is required parameter. Supports pagination using limit and offset.

**Deprecated endpoint - use GET /agent/:agentId instead.**

Gets a specific agent by its ID for the specified location with all its versions. locationId is required parameter. The agent must have active status.

404.description: Agent not found or not available

post.description:
**Deprecated endpoint - use POST /agent/:agentId/execute instead.**

Executes the specified agent and returns a non-streaming JSON response with the complete agent output. The agent must be in active status and belong to the specified location. locationId is required in the request body. 

**Session Management:**
- For the first message in a new session, do not include the `executionId` in the request payload.
- The API will return an `executionId` along with the agent response, which uniquely identifies this conversation session.
- To continue the conversation within the same session, include the `executionId` from the previous response in subsequent requests.

400.description: Agent is not active or invalid request - locationId is required

403.description: User does not have required scopes to execute this agent

bearer.name: Authorization
bearer.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account (OR) Private Integration Token of Sub-Account.

Location-Access.name: Authorization
Location-Access.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account (OR) Private Integration Token of Sub-Account.

Location-Access-Only.name: Authorization
Location-Access-Only.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account.

Agency-Access.name: Authorization
Agency-Access.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Agency (OR) Private Integration Token of Agency.

Agency-Access-Only.name: Authorization
Agency-Access-Only.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Agency.

description.example: AI agent specialized in handling customer inquiries and support tickets

version.description: Version data for the agent including nodes, edges, and configuration

message.example: Agent created successfully with staging version.

example.name: Customer Support Agent
example.description: AI agent specialized in handling customer inquiries and support tickets

description.example: Updated version with improved customer handling logic

nodes.description: Complete array of nodes for the agent workflow. Provide all nodes including unchanged ones.

edges.description: Complete array of edges connecting the nodes. Provide all edges including unchanged ones.

globalVariables.description: Global variables accessible throughout the agent workflow

inputVariables.description: Input variables required from user at execution time

runtimeVariables.description: Runtime variables generated during agent execution

globalConfig.description: Global configuration including prompts and settings

globalPrompt.currentPrompt: You are a helpful customer support assistant.

example.name: Updated Customer Support Agent
example.description: Updated AI agent with enhanced customer support capabilities

locationId.description: Location ID for authorization (cannot be updated)

description.example: Updated AI agent with enhanced customer support capabilities

userId.description: User ID performing the promotion action

userName.description: User name performing the promotion action

userEmail.description: User email performing the promotion action

message.example: Draft published to production successfully. New draft version created for future edits.

data.description: Result data with production and new draft version details

example.name: Marketing Assistant
example.description: AI agent specialized in marketing strategy and content creation

example.name: Customer Support Bot
example.description: AI agent for handling customer inquiries and support tickets

agent.description: Agent metadata with all active versions

message.description: Message to send to the agent
message.example: How can you help me with my marketing?

executionId.description: Unique session identifier that maintains conversational context across multiple interactions within the same agent session. Omit this field for the first message in a new session. Include the executionId returned from the previous response to maintain context in subsequent messages.

inputVariables.description: Input variables to pass to the agent. These should match the input variables defined in the agent configuration.

versionId.description: Published version ID to execute. If not provided, the latest published production version will be used.

contactId.description: Contact ID to associate with this execution. When provided, contact data will be hydrated and made available to the agent.

executionId.description: Unique session identifier that maintains conversational context across multiple interactions within the same agent session. Use this ID in subsequent requests to continue the conversation.

interactionId.description: Unique identifier for a single interaction cycle, consisting of one user input and the corresponding agent response. Each message exchange generates a new interactionId.

response.example: I can help you with various tasks...

nextExpectedInput.description: Expected input type for next interaction

goalCompletion.description: When end node is added in the graph, this will be true if the agent reached the end node in the graph
